GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Intimo is here to help you shop eco-friendly fashion staples", "In an effort to provide you with quality eco-friendly fashion staples, Intimo is incorporating TENCEL celebrated natural fibres into its beloved line of ready-to-wear. Gone are the days of wondering what your favourite items were made from, or where these products originated, as you can now ensure the items you purchase are ethically sourced and crafted within sustainable production processes. Designed using a combination of natural, renewable and sustainable fibres, Intimo's limited capsule releases incorporate the use of TENCEL branded fibres, Australian merino wool and linen, making them quality essentials that promise to prove the test of time. "An Intimo bra or ready-to-wear piece is not something you throw out at the end of the season," explained Intimo marketing manager, Amy Greetham, via a press release. "Each of our collections are carefully considered to ensure their lifespan justifies their price point," Greetham continued, highlighting the fact that with quality and comfort at their core, these styles transcend trends. "We commit to maintaining the quality, design and fit of our collections to ensure that our clients are able to wear them for years to come, without the need to replace them too often." From Intimo's form-flattering Convertible Singlet, which features an interchangeable scooped neckline at the front and v-neckline at the back, to the brand's must-have Essential Cardigan, which boasts a full-length hem complete with chic stylised side splits, a number of the designs available for purchase utilise TENCEL's Modal and Lyocell fibres. As such, these sustainable styles feature enhanced breathability, moisture absorption, long-lasting softness, and thermal regulation. Derived from certified sustainable wood sourced from sustainably managed forests, these 100 per cent bio-based Modal and Lyocell fibres with a botanic origin–which can be combined with other fibres such as cotton, wool, silk and etc. In order to enhance a fabric's functionality–are renewable raw materials that are both compostable and biodegradable, meaning they are able to be integrated back into nature. On top of ensuring that a number of products are of an eco-friendly nature, Intimo too promises that the garments produced meet industry-recognised WRAP or SA8000 and Oeko-Tex accreditations, which serve to certify that the manufacturers and materials used by the brand meet ethical and safety production standards. In addition, Intimo is committing to focus on sustainability across all sectors of the business, and has set out to help each customer by offering a consultation prior to purchase in an effort to reduce its carbon footprint via less returns and exchanges "There is certainly more we can do, and we have committed much of our energy to becoming even more sustainable across all sectors of our business as we build collections and processes for 2021 and 2022," explains Greetham. Want to learn more about TENCEL and their sustainability-driven #FeelsSoRight campaign with Intimo? Click here."
